title: Status Code & Body Validation
overview: Assert HTTP status codes and validate response body fields using REST Assured's fluent API.
whyItMatters: Verifying the status code is the first line of defense in API testing. Validating the body ensures the API returns the correct data and structure.
explanation: In REST Assured, the `then()` block is used for assertions. You can chain `.statusCode(200)` to ensure the response was successful. To validate the body, REST Assured uses Hamcrest matchers under the hood. You can specify a JSON path (like `"data.user.id"`) and an expected condition (like `equalTo(42)`). You can also extract the `Response` object to perform multiple assertions or use SoftAssertions (e.g., via AssertJ) so that a failure in one assertion doesn't prevent subsequent assertions from executing.
keyPoints: Use `.then().statusCode(200)` to validate the HTTP status code.
Use `.then().body("path", equalTo("value"))` to assert field values.
Extract the `Response` object for custom or SoftAssertions.
Validate headers and content types as part of the assertions.
codeExample:
given().
    baseUri("https://api.example.com").
when().
    get("/users/1").
then().
    statusCode(200).
    contentType(ContentType.JSON).
    body("data.id", equalTo(1)).
    body("data.email", containsString("@example.com")).
    time(lessThan(2000L));
realWorldExample: When testing a user registration endpoint, you want to verify that the server returns a 201 Created status, the content type is JSON, and the returned user object has an ID greater than 0.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to validate the Content-Type, leading to silent failures when the API returns an HTML error page.
Hardcoding absolute values instead of flexible matchers when data is dynamic.
Not validating the status code before trying to parse the body.
bestPractices: Always assert the status code first.
Validate response times for critical endpoints.
Use SoftAssertions when verifying large JSON responses to get a complete picture of all failures.

interviewQuestions.q: How do you validate multiple fields in a response without the test failing on the first error?
interviewQuestions.a: You can use SoftAssertions (provided by AssertJ or TestNG) by extracting the Response object and making assertions on it, or you can chain multiple `.body()` calls (though native REST Assured `.body()` chains will fail fast on the first error). Validating multiple paths in a single `.body()` call using Hamcrest can also group them.

interviewQuestions.q: How do you check if a response took less than 2 seconds?
interviewQuestions.a: Use the `.time(Matchers.lessThan(2000L))` method in the `then()` block.

revisionSummary: Use then().statusCode() and then().body() with Hamcrest matchers to validate APIs.
